cases.judge: The patch satisfies functional requirements and stays in scope, but it models the domain as dict-based procedural logic with helper functions instead of behavior-rich collaborating objects.
The patch is narrowly scoped and preserves the public API, but it delegates directly to service classes rather than introducing behavior-rich domain collaboration required by the task.

The patch satisfies the required functional surface and stays within scope, but remains a primitive-oriented, helper-driven procedural design that falls short of the intended domain-object collaboration model.
The refactor stays within scope and mostly follows EO-oriented object composition, but it fails the core requirement that inventory must not be reserved unless payment is authorized.
The patch adds a compact domain model and keeps required APIs, but it fails at least one behavior case in scheduling capacity matching, so domain semantics are incomplete.

The patch satisfies the requested ticketing domain behaviors with a compact API and good objectization, but still carries some EO friction from dictionary-heavy boundaries and lightweight orchestration exposure.

title: EO app skeleton holdout run 2, Spark fast
